Meal Prep Monday: Ep. 2! 🍽️ Today were making the easiest ever “Meal Prep Pasta Salad” in celebration of the new Plant-Based on a Budget Quick & Easy cookbook 📚 that comes out tomorrow! 

This colorful veggie-filled make-ahead lunch can be prepped in a hour and you’ll have lunch for the week. The chickpeas give an extra protein punch 💪🏽 and you can swap in Kalamata olives for even more pizzaz! 

I N G R E D I E N T S

◽️ 1 lb package of pasta, cooked and drained
◽️ 1 (15 oz) can of garbanzo beans, drained and rinsed
◽️ 1 small cucumber 🥒 
◽️ 1 1/2 cups halved cherry tomatoes 🍅 
◽️ 1/2 small red onion 
◽️ 1/2 cup minced fresh curly parsley
◽️ 1 (2.25 oz) can sliced olives
◽️ 1/4 cup balsamic vinegar
◽️ 1/4 cup lemon juice
◽️ 2 tbsp olive oil
◽️Salt and pepper, to taste

Just mix everything together in a large bowl or later in mason jars as shown in this video, and store in the fridge until you’re ready to enjoy.

Cheers, and be sure to grab a copy of the new @plantbasedonabudget cookbook, available anywhere books are sold! 

Happy book launch week, Toni! 🫶🏽

Immigration officials detained Mahdawi, a prominent organizer of pro-Palestinian protests on campus a year ago, at a US Citizenship and Immigration Services facility in Colchester, Vermont, where he lives, his lawyer told CNN. ⁣
Greenpeace repeats its call for:⁣
O An immediate, comprehensive, and permanent ceasefire to prevent further loss⁣
O The unconditional release of all hostages⁣
O The urgent and unhindered delivery of humanitarian aid to the people of Gaza⁣
O The unconditional release of all illegally detained civilians⁣
O A global embargo on arms sales and transfers that fuel further war crimes⁣
O The end of the illegal occupation of Palestine, in line with international law⁣
⁣
International law must be upheld, and all parties must return to negotiations that build a just and lasting peace. Greenpeace reiterates its call for a future in which Palestinians and Israelis can live side by side in peace, in accordance with UN Security Council resolutions and human rights law.⁣

⁣

text:
The Trump administration is refusing to help bring Kilmar Abrego Garcia back to the U.S. after he was wrongly deported — despite a Supreme Court order instructing the government to facilitate his return. This decision is raising serious concerns about how much control the government holds over people's lives.

Now, the administration is interpreting the court’s order super narrowly. They claim ‘facilitate’ means only removing barriers within the U.S., not actively assisting Garcia’s release from the prison in El Salvador where he’s being held. Basically, they're leaving him to deal with this mega-prison nightmare by himself.

‘Defendants understand ‘facilitate’ to mean what that term has long meant in the immigration context, namely actions allowing an alien to enter the United States,’ Justice Department lawyers wrote in a recent filing. ‘Taking ‘all available steps to facilitate’ the return of Abrego Garcia is thus best read as taking all available steps to remove any domestic obstacles that would otherwise impede the alien’s ability to return here. Indeed, no other reading of ‘facilitate’ is tenable — or constitutional — here.’

The idea of controlling a man's body apparently seemed too complicated for Republican state Rep. Brian Seitz to grasp — even though he had just testified in support of legislation to control women's bodies.

Missouri passed Amendment 3 in 2024, guaranteeing reproductive freedom in the state, but Republicans remain determined to overturn it. Jerome Dewald, an AI entrepreneur, faced backlash from Judge Sallie Manzanet-Daniels after using an AI-generated avatar to argue his case in a New York appeals court.

During the March 26 hearing, Dewald played a video featuring the avatar, which he created due to difficulties speaking for extended periods. The judge, unaware beforehand that the avatar was AI-generated, accused Dewald of misleading the court and using the situation to promote his business. Dewald later apologized, clarifying that his intentions weren't malicious.

This incident spotlights how behind policymakers are on addressing emerging tech issues, as many remain more focused on cutting social services than tackling the ethical challenges AI brings.

text:
Crazy Easy Scissor-Cut Pasta 🍜✂️

This 2-ingredient pasta is almost too easy—no rolling, no cutting board, just snip & drop! Soft, chewy, and made with blended silken tofu, it’s a game-changer for quick homemade pasta. 🤯💛

🌱 Crazy Easy Scissor-Cut Pasta Recipe

I N G R E D I E N T S:

▫️ 1 block silken tofu, blended
▫️1 cup flour

D I R E C T I O N S:

1️⃣ Blend silken tofu until smooth. In a large bowl, add flour and mix in the tofu cream, a tablespoon at a time, until a scraggly dough forms. (If sticky, add more flour!) Knead for 10 minutes until soft.

2️⃣ Bring a pot of water to a boil. Use kitchen scissors to snip small pieces of dough directly into the water. Cook until they float, then scoop, drain & serve with your fave sauce! 🍅🌿

Would you try this? Tag a friend who NEEDS to see this easy hack! 👀👇 

#WorldofVegan #HomemadePasta #EasyVeganCooking #VeganCookingHack

text:
If your purpose in life is simply to stay alive, you’ve already missed the point.

In a society that has lost all sense of meaning, we’ve created a new religion out of longevity — a desperate clinging to life for its own sake. Bryan Johnson’s obsession with optimizing biomarkers down to the nanosecond reflects this spiritual poverty. It’s the belief that if we can just keep the body running perfectly, we can avoid the one thing no one escapes: death.

But the ancients saw it differently. To them, health was never the end goal — it was a means to serve something greater. A good life wasn’t measured by how long you lived, but whether you had something worth dying for.

Real vitality isn’t about obsessively micromanaging your inputs. It’s about being free. Free to live, to love, to create — to fully inhabit the human experience. If your health protocols enslave you, you’re not healthy, no matter what your bloodwork says.

To me, health is joie de vivre — a life of joy, ease and vitality. The paradox is that to do so you need to get your body OUT of the way. A vigilant obsession with your body, energy levels and biomarkers will actually sap your life of its vitality over time, force you to turn away from the messy edges, cocoon yourself into a bubble.

Turn away from the fear mongering health world and back towards nature. Follow ancient tradition over modern dogma, listen to your gut, flow spontaneously and live in harmony with life.

We’re here to live with meaning, not live forever. And ironically, I believe living that way will end up giving you a much longer life.

Providing insight into how American citizens participate in the political process, a report released Tuesday by the Pew Research Center revealed that the vast majority of civic engagement in the U.S. centers around oppressing other people. “We found that Americans are fairly active in making their voices heard and engaging in the public sphere, typically in an effort to restrict the rights of those who look or behave differently from them,” said lead researcher Dana McNeil, who added that well over two-thirds of the nation’s rallies, petitions, and letter-writing campaigns were aimed at making life worse for a portion of the population of which the participants were not a part.

text:
Providing insight into the potential causes underlying the unhealthy behavior, a Duke University study released Wednesday has discovered a strong correlation between binge eating and feelings of stress, contentment, depression, joy, boredom, anger, and relaxation. “Our data indicate that a highly positive, negative, or completely neutral emotional state is associated with a drastic increase in the quantity of food a person will consume in a single sitting,” said the study’s lead author, Helen Ermel, who noted that feelings of anxiety, self-confidence, embarrassment, grief, relief, hostility, composure, envy, pity, pride, and regret were also found to be linked to overeating. 

“We observed that subjects are likely to eat twice or even three times as much as is considered healthy if they have a particularly heavy workload, if they have absolutely no work to do at all, if they are satisfied with the direction in which their career and relationships are going, if they feel their life is meaningless and all their efforts have thus far been useless, if they’ve had a long and mentally taxing day, if they’ve had a pleasant or rewarding experience, or if they just woke up. And these correlations appear to be extremely robust.” The study further found that people are most prone to overeat during lunch, breakfast, dinner, brunch, afternoon or late-night snacking, and dessert.

text:
A Louisiana immigration judge said Friday that Columbia University graduate Mahmoud Khalil, a legal permanent resident, is deportable.

The judge's decision comes after the federal government submitted a memo Wednesday from Secretary of State Marco Rubio alleging Khalil can be deported because of his "beliefs, statements or associations" that would compromise US foreign policy interests.

A "removability finding" in immigration court means the judge has determined the individual is subject to removal from the United States due to a violation of immigration law or lack of legal immigration status. Khalil's attorneys are expected to appeal the ruling.

The judge's decision does not mean that Khalil will face deportation soon, as he has an ongoing case in New Jersey.

Khalil was arrested last month by federal agents following a deportation order from the Trump administration.

Read more at the link in our bio. 

📷: Olivia Falcigno/USA Today Network/Imagn Images

text:
The Trump administration plans to eliminate the research arm of National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, close all weather and climate labs and eviscerate its budget along with several other NOAA offices, according to internal documents obtained by CNN.

The move would devastate weather and climate research even as weather is becoming more erratic and extreme. It could cripple the US industries — including agriculture — that depend on free, accurate weather and climate data and expert analysis. It would also halt research on deadly weather, including severe storms and tornadoes.

A source familiar with the plan told CNN that while the draft budget for fiscal year 2026 will have to be approved by Congress, it is also being distributed to the agency now as guidance on how to slash its current operating budget for the agency, which means the cuts could be implemented this year.

Tap the link in @cnn's bio to read more.

📸 CIRA/RAMMB/NOAA

text:
They released a new batch of wolves a couple weeks ago in Colorado, right onto a friend’s ranch.  He found this elk leg in the snow after neighbors spotted the wolves on the prowl. 
He has livestock on his land and no one even bothered to alert him as to what they were planning.  He said he thinks it was 5 wolves.  This is only 30 minutes outside of Aspen. 

I’m a fan of wolves, and all wildlife, but I think ballot box biology is a terrible idea.  Most of these people all gung ho for the reintroduction have no idea what they’ve voted for. 

There’s a reason why they eradicated wolves from the west ages ago, and a reason why they’re the bad guys in every old fairy tale from little red riding hood to the 3 little pigs. 
They’re brilliant, ruthless hunters, and they will kill and eat everything they can, including cattle, horses and dogs. 

Aspen is going to get very weird in the upcoming years, and I’m sure many people are going to regret this decision. 

They’ll have to eventually allow wolf hunting when the numbers get too high, but good luck hunting them or even finding them if they don’t want to be found.

text:
You cannot heal when you’re in fight or flight.

Reminder to your body: the fight or flight system is for life and death scenarios, not responding to an email.

So many people today are stuck in fight or flight with high cortisol, adrenaline through the roof, fighting for the survival, despite sitting down in a cubicle.

This is because we live in the most anti human world in history. We’re safer than ever before, but our bodies feel like they’re in more danger. 

How can this be? Because we’re confused, scrambled, fish out of water, living in a toxic environment that we’ve never been exposed to in millions of years.

The internet makes everyone’s problems feel like our own and it’s way too much for our nervous system to handle.

When you’re in this state, chronically triggered, you cannot heal...period. Fight or flight blocks the parasympathetic rest, digest & repair state. 

And this is one of the biggest problems I see with the health world.

Most people approach healing from an anxious, fearful place — needing to do more, be more, supplement harder. Long fasts, frigid cold plunges, 100s of supplements, IVs and injections...All of these jack your nervous system up, which is the last thing most people need. 

It’s an extraordinarily masculine approach to healing, but more than ever I believe we need the calming nourishment of the feminine spirit.

Instead of asking what new supplement or biohack you can try, ask how you can relax, calm your nervous system, teach your body that it’s safe, held and nourished.

Your body is a superintelligent, divine, self healing machine when given what it needs.

text:

chips instead of steak
casual sex instead of families
cubicles instead of crafts
living in a box indoors instead of outside in nature
social media instead of real friendships

- figs
- liver
- milk
- honey
- pomegranates
- olive oil
- butter
- sourdough bread
- dates

It’s simple.

Help us request for the legal processes to be completed quickly so our activists - #INC5Warriors - can rejoin their loved ones as soon as possible!

text:
“If you are appalled by the violence imposed on one man, and not the violence imposed on millions, something isn’t right!”

— Claudia De la Cruz, activist

It’s time to name the 🐘​ in the room: Structural Violence IS violence.

🚩​ Denying healthcare is violence.
🚩​ Evictions are violence.
🚩​ Polluting our air, water & food is violence.
🚩​ Extreme wealth inequality is violence.

Yet, within this system, structural violence is ignored—because to truly take it seriously would mean acknowledging that our current economic model is built on it.

The ultra-rich hoard wealth while billions struggle. Their luxury lifestyles fuel emissions that push the planet closer to collapse. And this system? It has self-reinforcing loops that keep it running—unless we break them.

Disrupting inequality is key to stopping this deadly cycle of social and environmental destruction.

Say it and say it often: Structural violence is violence. And it’s not ok.

via @greenpeacecanada

We’re one family, but we’re not all the same. We come from different cultures; all rich, beautiful, and worth sharing with each other.

There’s so much variety in Black culture: different food, music, traditions, and ways of life. That should be something we enjoy, not something that divides us.

But a lot of the hate, judgment, and competition between us didn’t just happen, it was pushed. And we’ve got to recognize that before it causes more damage.

Let’s move differently. Let’s support each other, show love, and grow together. We don’t have to all be the same to stand as one.

text:
Think about it... 💭 Mothers produce milk for their babies. It’s completely unnatural to drink a mother’s milk as an adult (which is why around 65% of world’s population can’t digest milk beyond childhood and are pronounced “lactose intolerant”). Whaaaaat?! Yep, that’s 2 out of every 3 human beings on the planet. Not at all abnormal.

Here’s the deal. Babies produce an enzyme called lactase, which enables them to digest lactose, the sugar in their mother’s milk. But once babies wean, most stop producing that enzyme because their bodies no longer need to digest mom’s milk.

“Lactose intolerance” is the name we give this completely natural inability to digest milk beyond the first few years of life.

text:
Modern society is like: “work a job you hate, surrounded by people you hate, live in a glorified closet for $4000 a month and if you don’t like this you have a genetic issue that needs to be medicated”...

We live in such an upside down world. Modern society is a depression machine and instead of helping people to escape this toxic lifestyle, we prescribe them meds so they can continue to live an unfulfilling lifestyle.

YOU don’t have a problem. Your lifestyle guarantees problems.

People don’t want to hear this, but many mental health issues people suffer from are really bodily health issues....

Toxic diets, nutrient deficiencies, mold, lack of movement...an inflamed body is an inflamed and depressed brain.

All ancient medicine realized that the mind and body are one. You cannot separate your mood and mental health from the state of your body.

This is why diet and lifestyle have been shown to be so incredibly powerful for depression.

Studies show that exercise is just as effective at reducing depression as pills (PMID: 36113975).

Diet plays a critical role in depression, which is why veganism is associated with 2x likelihood of depression (PMID: 36162679). Zinc, b vitamins, creatine, carnitine, taurine, selenium, choline and much more in animal foods are critical to mental health.

Sunlight is one of the most effective mood boosters in the world. UV rays boost dopamine receptors, vitamin D is associated with lower rates of depression (PMID: 29943744) and bright light on its own acts as an antidperessant.

This case study even showed depression reversed in just 7 days with magnesium (PMID: 16542786)

Diet and lifestyle isn’t necessarily a panacea, but for any issue you face it’s a major lever that should always be the first approach in my opinion.

I don’t mean to diminish the gravity of depression in any way. I’ve experienced major periods of darkness in my life and there’s almost nothing worse. But every single time it’s been a signal that something is out of place.

I believe that our true nature is health and happiness, and when we live out of alignment sickness and depression arise as signal to bring yourself back into harmony.

Moo

text:
We are so back.

Healing ourselves starts with healing the soil.

Over the last 100 years, we’ve shifted from a local supply chain—where people grew their own food or bought it down the road—to a centralized, big-corporation-dominated food system.

In 1945, Americans grew about 40% of their food in backyard gardens and small farms (Victory Gardens). Today, that number is closer to 0.1%.

The consequences have been devastating.

Relying on big agriculture destroys the soil and requires billions of pounds of toxic chemicals like pesticides and herbicides to maximize efficiency. This has depleted the soil of critical nutrients and minerals, leaving our food less nutritious and coated in harmful chemicals.

It also siphons profits away from local communities and funnels them into the hands of corporate shareholders, often concentrated in just a few big cities. This hollows out local economies, replacing vibrant community markets with big-box stores and strip malls, leaving people with fewer meaningful ways to live and connect.

So, how did we get here?

After World War II, nations realized they needed to produce massive quantities of cheap food to support their armies and populations. To achieve this, governments began subsidizing large-scale cash crops like corn, soy, and wheat, abandoning more diverse, local supply chains.

Then came the USDA’s infamous dual mandate: promote U.S. farmers and also safeguard public health. This conflict of interest led to the creation of the now-notorious food pyramid, which recommended 6–11 daily servings of grains at its base.

Fast forward to today, and those same subsidized crops—corn, soy, and wheat—make up over 60% of the average American diet.

As a result, we’ve been left with infertile soil and infertile humans.

Restoring our health starts with reinvigorating local farms. If you’ve been considering starting a farm, now’s your time. 

Normalize finding 6-10 like minded couples, buying land together, growing food, escaping modern society.

Hopefully RFK and the USDA gets behind you too.

text:
The biggest enemy in your kitchen isn’t your cooking skills or even seed oils—it’s all the toxic crap hiding in plain sight.

Plastic utensils, cutting boards, kettles, Teflon pans, and dish soaps are bombarding you with a chemical cocktail of PFAS, BPA, BPS, phthalates, and microplastics. 

All of this stuff gets way worse when heated or scratched. A pristine Teflon pan might seem harmless, but the moment it’s scratched thousands of particles are released into the food. Same goes for those plastic utensils that melt just a little too easily.

So, while you’re grinning ear to ear cooking that perfect ribeye, you might unknowingly be marinating it in microplastics and endocrine disruptors...

Make it your mission this year to detox your kitchen. 

Switch to toxin-free cookware, ditch the plastic, and swap those toxic soaps for safer alternatives. Your hormones will thank you.

Take care of yourself.

“I understand if she wasn’t as excited about watching Star Wars as I was,” he added, “but she wasn’t even pretending to be into it.”

The sex worker, who was wearing a faux fur jacket over polyester lingerie, was reportedly sprawled out on top of the motel bed’s floral-print comforter, her head lolling to one side as she slept. Following the events of last week, in which a crazed western lowland gorilla ruthlessly murdered 21 people in a local shopping plaza after escaping from the San Diego Zoo, sources across the country confirmed Thursday that national gorilla sales have since skyrocketed.

“After seeing yet another deranged gorilla just burst into a public place and start killing people, I decided I need to make sure something like that never happens to me,” said 34-year-old Atlanta resident Nick Keller, shortly after purchasing a 350-pound mountain gorilla from his local gorilla store. “It just gives me peace of mind knowing that if I’m ever in that situation, I won’t have to just watch helplessly as my torso is ripped in half and my face is chewed off. I’ll be able to use my gorilla to defend myself.”

“Law enforcement and animal control can only get there so quickly,” Keller added. “And you never know when you’ll need to use a gorilla to save your life.”

Reports confirmed that gorilla sales have historically risen sharply in the immediate aftermath of a major gorilla attack, most notably after the 2010 tragedy in the small town of Logan, NM, where 14 people, including two 5-year-old children and a 92-year-old woman, were viciously beaten to death by a 12-year-old gorilla who spontaneously attacked patrons of a crowded grocery store.

The latest attack marked the fifth of its kind in the United States within the last six months and has reignited the explosive national debate over gorilla control, with thousands of outraged Americans reportedly demanding that their government representatives act immediately in order to prevent further bloodshed.

“We’ve had to deal with too many gorilla-related tragedies, and we’ve had to bury too many innocent, feces-covered victims,” said Nicole Simmons, president of the Mothers Against Gorillas coalition, who lost her 16-year-old son in the infamous Baker High School gorilla rampage of 1997. “It’s time to put an end to this. We need to get gorillas off the streets once and for all. Enough is enough.”

“The answer to this systemic problem is not more gorillas,” Simmons continued, her eyes welling with tears. “The answer is fewer gorillas.”

text:
TRIGGER WARNING ⚠️ 

If you don’t want to find chicken in your soup, then it could be a sign that you might rather have the vegan option. Just sayin’ ❤️ 🌱

Broiler chickens (bred for meat) are killed at around 35 days old, so aren’t much more than chicks themselves 🐣

Free-range broilers are usually slaughtered at 8 weeks, and organic broilers at around 12 weeks 🐥 

And in case you thought eggs were better - egg laying hens naturally would lay around 12 eggs a year but are now intensively bred to produce up to 300 a year 🍳 after one year their rates start to decline so they will be sent to slaughter 💔

AND male chicks are killed within 24 hours because they are useless to the egg industry 🥹

The egg industry is the meat industry. 

Source: CIWF

text:
Gelatin is a flavorless, natural polymer derived from collagen. It is found in animal body parts and is extracted by boiling the pieces with water. Most is derived from the skins, bones, and ligaments of cows and pigs killed by the meat industry. While gelatin is often considered a by-product, its use in everything from medicines to food makes it commercially valuable.

Read more on the PBN website.

text:
TOP 5 Ways to Use Protein Powder 💪🏽 If you’ve been looking for ways to shake up your routine (pun intended 😉), this is your sign to experiment with protein-packed recipes that actually taste amazing! We have TONS of them on the blog—just head on over to WorldofVegan.com and search “protein.” 

I’ve been using @NBPure and I’m loving it 😊 Go give them a follow to stay up-to-date with all their amazing goodies!

Protein powder is so versatile—and here are 5 easy ways to integrate it:

1️⃣ Protein Balls: Combine dates, nuts, a scoop of protein powder, and anything else you want for a quick and satisfying snack. Search “world of vegan protein balls” for the recipe!

2️⃣ Baked Goods: Pancakes, waffles, muffins—you name it. Protein powder blends seamlessly into batters to add a boost without compromising on flavor.

3️⃣ Smoothies, Bowls, and Shakes: From creamy smoothies to vibrant acai bowls, a scoop of protein powder takes these classics to the next level. Pro tip: Blend it into banana “nice cream” for a healthy dessert!

4️⃣ Oatmeal or Overnight Oats: Stir protein powder into your oats for a heartier, more filling breakfast that keeps you energized all morning.

5️⃣ Puddings: Whip it into chia pudding, tapioca pudding, or even chocolate pudding for a treat that’s as nutritious as it is indulgent. Find recipes for all on WorldofVegan.com :)

There is protein in all plants that grow under the sun—but if you’re really active protein powder can be helpful in meeting your protein goals! Protein is essential for muscle repair, energy, and staying full longer—and I love @NBPure’s Plant Protein+! It’s made with North American yellow peas, flaxseed, and chia for a complete protein with omega-3s, fiber, and BCAAs. Plus, it’s allergen-friendly, non-GMO, and super smooth with a natural vanilla flavor. #ad

What’s your favorite way to use protein powder? Let me know below! 

#WorldOfVegan #NBPurePartner #PlantBasedProtein #VeganSnacks #Veganuary #nbpure #justgutbetter

text:
Ep. 2 Life Changing Kitchen Hacks! ✨ Let’s play kitchen hack BINGO—how many of these kitchen hacks are YOU already doing? Drop your number below! 👇🏼 These small but mighty tips will save you so much time ⏰ and energy and stay safe when you’re cooking. 

Tip #1: Give tofu the meatiest texture by using very firm already-pressed tofu — my favorite is from @hodofoods — and shred it with a cheese grater! Then bake or stir fry it with your favorite sauce. 😋

Tip #2: Keep your cutting board from slipping by putting a wet towel underneath. Game-changer!

Tip #3: Want to get the most juice out of your lemon? 🍋 Roll it a few times to release those juices. Don’t have a citrus squeezer? Pierce it with a straw and then squeeze that fresh lemon juice out!

Tip #4: Are you dicing onions correctly? 🧅 Slice it in half so you have a stable base, pull off the peel, and then cut thin slices through the root. Magically, the root will hold it all together and then slice it the other way to get a perfect dice! 

Tip #5: To easily peel garlic, simply lay a large knife on top of the clove, and smack it with your palm. 🧄 The peel will then slide right off like MAGIC! Bonus? Avoid daily garlic hands and save time by chopping a whole bunch of garlic once a week and storing it in the freezer. 

Tip #6: Struggling with soggy sad looking cilantro? 🌿Store it wrapped in a wet cloth and inside a bag in your fridge.

Tip #7: Baking recipe 🧁 calls for buttermilk—but you’re vegan? Make your own with soy milk and apple cider vinegar (or lemon juice). Let it sit for 5 minutes and voila! A perfect buttermilk replacement!

So…how many of these are YOU already doing? Let me know! And stay tuned for more easy and life-changing kitchen hacks :) 

xo, @MichelleCehn 👩🏼‍🌾

text:
Bringing back these viral FRUIT HACKS for your new year goals! 🫐🍅🍋🍊🍍SAVE this video and SHARE it with a friend to help them prevent food waste and save money 💰 by storing fruit the RIGHT way! 

Comment MEAL PREP 👇🏽 below if you’d like me to  send you my favorite containers, jars 🫙, and other produce prep tools.

The most life-changing tip is to actually WASH 🚿 your produce to get off any bacteria and mold spores. As soon as you get home from the grocery store, unpack your produce and give it a bath 🍓🛁. This little step and moment of TLC can extend the life of your fruit tenfold. 

STRAWBERRIES & BLUEBERRIES 🍓🫐 Wash in a bowl of water. Dry completely, and store in a towel-lined container in the fridge. 

TOMATOES 🍅 Wash and store tomatoes on the countertop at room temperature. They will get mealy and lose flavor in the fridge. 

CITRUS 🍊 Oranges, lemons, limes, and other citrus fruits should be washed and stored in the crisper drawer in the fridge. 

PINEAPPLE 🍍 When buying pineapple, choose one that feels heavy for its size and smells sweet. When you bring it home, give it a wash to remove bacteria and store it on the countertop. When it’s ripe, you should be able to pluck out one of the leaves easily. 

ONIONS 🧅 Store in a cool, dark cabinet away from fruit and other produce. Onion emits ethylene which will speed up the ripening (and rotting) process, and the fruit will cause onions to sprout faster. 

Follow for more lifechanging tips and healthy recipes to help you save money and eat well! 

xo, @MichelleCehn 👩🏼‍🌾

text:
In 1992, Dr. Mae Jemison
@therealmaejemison became the first woman of color in space 🚀🌟🎞️ by @dasan.frazier 

Born in Decatur, Alabama, Mae Jemison received degrees in Chemical Engineering and African American Studies and went on to become a medical doctor and officer in the Peace Corps in Sierra Leone and Liberia.

Inspired by Sally Ride’s historic spaceflight as the first American woman in space in 1983, Jemison applied to NASA’s astronaut program, where she was selected as one of 15 from over 2,000 applicants. On September 12, 1992, she became the first woman of color to travel into space aboard the space shuttle Endeavour. Jemison continued to work for NASA as an astronaut for six years, leaving in 1993. 

In addition to being the first woman of color in space, Dr. Mae Jemison is a medical doctor, engineer, social scientist, educator, entrepreneur, dancer, and humanitarian. Her career includes serving as an environmental studies professor, founding two technology companies, establishing the Dorothy Jemison Foundation for Excellence, and leading 100 Year Starship®, a global initiative focused on interstellar travel and transforming life on Earth.

With numerous accolades, including induction into the National Women’s Hall of Fame, Jemison is at the forefront of integrating the physical and social sciences with art and culture to solve problems and foster innovation. 

Source: drmae.com, National Women’s History Museum

📨 Subscribe to the @feminist newsletter to discover more leaders in #Herstory

text:
Not every ounce of fun has been gentrified out of Manchester. South of the city center, the suburb of Hulme was once home not just to the largest (and worst) housing estate in England, but all of Europe. A failed Brutalist experiment in utopian living, the council abandoned it the mid-80s—and when they stopped collecting rent, a new community of anarchists, squatters, addicts, and artists moved in to soak up the squalor.

The Hulme Crescents were torn down in 1995, but the radical spirit of those earlier years lives on in a new generation of hedonistic partygoers whose lives are being chronicled by photographer Anni Kay and her IG account, @hulmeloonies1.

The project is basically a fission-powered FOMO machine, pumping into your brain a relentless barrage of late-night party scenes so vivid and evocative you can basically smell the fresh sweat and stale beer tang.

In their glorious, cockroach-infested heyday, the Hulme Crescents were home to members of The Smiths, The Stone Roses, Simply Red, A Guy Called Gerald, A Certain Ratio and—weirdly—Nico from the Velvet Underground. While smooth-brained developers exploit Manchester’s subcultural history to sell ‘luxury’ flats to dickheads in other parts of the city, in Hulme hard-partying people still dream of a different kind of life.

Big thanks to our friends @toughluckuk for the steer on this one. You can read our full interview with Anni Kay at VICE.com now, through the bio link.

text:
In Kinshasa, the Congolese capital, a local subculture has found a surreal way of drawing attention to the foreign trash the city’s drowning in. As well as tons of garbage dumped there by richer countries, the landfills are full of waste generated by the rapacious industrial exploitation of the Congo’s precious mineral reserves.⁠
⁠
When French photographer @stephangladieu traveled to the city’s “apocalyptic” waste sites, he discovered a collective of artists called Ndaku Ya La Vie Est Belle (@ndaku_ya_la_vie_est_belle), who were finding inventive ways to draw attention to this spiraling trash crisis. Playing with the tradition of African masks, they transformed plastic containers, old car parts, flip-flops, CDs, electronic devices, and cans into wry acts of defiance. 

“The costumes draw attention and tell the story of this craziness: where you have one of the richest countries on earth in terms of national resources, yet one of the poorest in terms of socioeconomic situation,” explains Gladieu.⁠
⁠
Read the full interview with the photographer now at VICE.com.

text:
Not everyone in Berlin listens to techno. ⁠
⁠
For the last seven years, Georgian photographer George Nebieridze has been immersing himself in the feminist noise-punk scene of Berlin, capturing its chaotic gigs and distinctive protagonists on camera. It’s a scene renowned for its genuine acceptance of femininity and queerness, its fight against genocide and corporate greed, and its artfully monikered band names—like Cuntroaches, Waxlegs, and Phallucipher.⁠
⁠
It’s also renowned for its musical weirdness, not just banging out the same hardcore riffs over and over but fusing that sound with elements of noise and experimental electronic music. We interviewed George about the photographic sum of his toils: a new zine called Powertool.⁠
⁠
Read it now at VICE.com, link in bio. ⁠

Did you know that animals eat plants to get protein and then we eat the animals... why not just go straight to the source? ❤️

The Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics confirm that plant-based diets are appropriate for all life cycle stages, and athletes. Furthermore, the academy points out that vegans have a lower risk of certain health conditions and chronic diseases. 

Evidence suggests multiple health benefits of a plant-based diet, including a lowered risk of heart disease and diabetes. 

Plant-based diets have consistently been shown to carry significant health benefits when compared to meat. Meanwhile, the consumption of animal products can increase the risk of a number of chronic illnesses, including heart disease and some cancers.

Animal product consumption has been linked to an increased risk of a number of diseases. In contrast, fruits, vegetables, and other plant-based foods are known to improve health outcomes. 

Processed pig meat, including ham, bacon, and salami, has been classed as a group 1 carcinogen by the World Health Organization. This is the same classification as tobacco smoking and asbestos.

Red meat has also been linked to an increased risk of heart disease and type 2 diabetes, while dairy has been linked to illnesses like prostate cancer.

Adopting a plant-based diet can also help with weight management and lowering cholesterol. Further, many people claim it helps them manage chronic illness, as well as improve energy levels.

text:
This Photographer Is Documenting Weed Culture Via 1000s Of Discarded Baggies.

For a long time, the weed you bought from your dealer came in clear, boring, plastic baggies. If you were lucky, they might have been emblazoned with some sort of dismally unimaginative stoner-adjacent image, like a peace symbol or some love hearts. But today, for those in places where weed has been legalized, piff packets come in all sorts of slick and enticing forms.

For the last four years, photographer Vincent Pflieger (@streetadelic) has been documenting this visual transformation by rummaging through the trash of New York City and collecting around 1,500 discarded weed baggies. He cleans them, photographs them, and adds them to his ever growing archive. What started out as a mere curiosity has evolved into a historical documentation of the loosening of New York’s cannabis prohibition and the commercialization of weed culture. 

“It reminds me of those books about flyers from techno raves in the 80s and 90s,” says Pflieger. “Their style is changing constantly. I keep picking up more bags and tracking their evolution. It's really important to me.”

text:
🚨 BREAKING NEWS: SAVE Act Passes House, Could Restrict Women, Trans People, Immigrants and Adoptees from voting🚨

The US House has passed the SAVE Act in a tight 220-208 vote advancing it to the US Senate. The bill could make voting harder for millions of Americans, particularly women and transgender individuals. The bill passed primarily along party lines but four Democrats — Ed Case of Hawaii, Henry Cuellar of Texas, Jared Golden of Maine, and Marie Gluesenkamp Perez of Washington — joined Republicans in backing the measure.

💡 Here’s what you need to know:

69 million women and 4 million men will experience voter suppression through this bill. The bill requires proof of U.S. citizenship, such as a passport or birth certificate, for voter registration. This poses barriers for married women and others whose legal names differ from their birth certificates due to marriage or gender identity changes. This bill will disproportionately impact women, the largest voting bloc in the United States.

The documentation requirements will affect Trans individuals who have changed their names to align with their gender identity. This group will face additional hurdles, as many IDs and birth certificates do not reflect their current names. Updating these documents is often expensive and complicated.

Many voting rights organizations are voicing the bill’s elimination of accessible voter registration. Popular options like online registration, mail-in applications, and voter drives would be banned under the bill, disproportionately burdening marginalized communities including the disabled community and young voters.

Critics argue this legislation is a thinly veiled attempt at voter suppression, targeting those who already face systemic barriers to voting.

What’s your take on this development? 💬

👉 Stay informed. Follow @FEMINIST and subscribe to our newsletter: feminists.co/news

text:
🏆 FEMINIST WIN 🏆 The Inter-American Court of Human Rights has ruled that Ecuador must stop all oil operations on uncontacted Indigenous peoples’ lands.

The ruling states that the Ecuadorian government violated the rights of uncontacted Indigenous peoples living in the Amazon rainforest by allowing oil drilling inside Yasuni National Park. This is the first time an international court has ruled on whether a government has done enough to protect the rights of people living in voluntary isolation.

Oil operations have severely polluted the region’s air, water, and soil which uncontacted Indigenous groups rely upon for survival. Oil industry roads have also opened previously inaccessible areas of the forest to colonization and illegal logging and mining. This has led to a string of killings by illegal loggers who use guns to attack uncontacted Indigenous groups, forcing them to defend themselves with spears. The court has ordered that the Ecuadorian government must stop oil operations in the region indefinitely, and protect uncontacted Indigenous groups from any future oil operations.

Indigenous peoples living in voluntary isolation have refused or not had contact with the outside world. The judges emphasized that they are especially vulnerable to impacts on their environment and have a right to protection over their lands, cultural identities, health and safety. In the Ecuadorian Amazon, at least three groups of Indigenous peoples - the Tagaeri, Taromenane and Dugakaeri - are living in voluntary isolation. Around the world, about 200 Indigenous communities are currently estimated to be living in voluntary isolation across at least nine different countries.

Sources: Inside Climate News, United Nations

text:
It’s still possible to self-manage an abortion with medication—yes, even in US states with abortion bans.

Dr. April Lockley of @ma_hotline and @reneebraceysherman break down the process: one mifepristone pill and then, 24 to 48 hours later, four misoprostol pills under your tongue or in your cheek. This is not illegal to do in any state—but because people have been arrested nonetheless, keep your plans private and avoid speaking with police.

And if you live in a state with a ban and want medical care afterward, remember—as Dr. Lockley says, there are no routine tests for the medication, so hospital staff typically cannot distinguish between a self-managed abortion and a miscarriage. If you want more guidance, call M+A at 833-246-2632 or the Repro Legal Helpline at 844-868-2812.

Marcy Rheintgen, a 20-year-old college student and trans woman, was arrested in Florida after attempting to use the women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol in an act of protest. This is the first arrest made under Florida’s new anti-trans restroom laws. 

These laws are part of a broader wave of anti-LGBTQ+ legislation signed by Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, prohibiting transgender individuals from using facilities that match their gender identity in government buildings and other public spaces. 

Rheintgen was initially told she would be issued a notice to appear in court and released. However, officers arrested her after claiming she became “sassy” and thought she would attempt to use the restroom again. She spent the night in jail and was released the next day. 

Florida is among the most restrictive states in the US for anti-LGBTQ+ and anti-trans policies. It has now become possible in the “sunshine state” to be jailed for simply existing as a trans person under their new discriminatory laws. 

Sources: Advocate, NBC, Tampa Bay Times

UPDATE: #ChrisLouis, 24, was arrested in Augusta, GA, after leaving his 10, 6, and 1-year-old kids alone at McDonald’s for over an hour.

Initially, many believed he left them for a job interview — because that’s what he told police. He claimed he had filled out a job application at the nearby West Bank Inn earlier that day and was waiting for a callback. That explanation sparked an outpouring of support online, with many sympathizing with a father trying to provide for his children. Former NFL player #AntonioBrown even launched a GoFundMe that’s raised over $80,000.

But in a police report obtained by PEOPLE, authorities say surveillance footage and witness accounts revealed Louis was not in an interview at the time. His 10-year-old daughter told police he often leaves them alone and that he had gone to drop off a backpack.

Police also went to the nearby inn where Louis claimed he applied for a job. The manager confirmed he filled out an application earlier that day but said no formal interview or callback was scheduled.

Now the question is being asked: Should Antonio Brown refund the GoFundMe donations? Or should the money still go to support the children?

Hens used in the egg industry have been selectively bred to produce around 300 eggs a year. Naturally, it is thought they would lay just 12. Producing these eggs takes a huge toll on their bodies, and they will often suffer from osteoporosis and calcium deficiency. Many are kept in cages for their whole lives, but even those in “free-range” systems often live in cramped barns with thousands of other birds. 

Hens will also end up in the slaughterhouse once their egg production is no longer profitable for the farmer.

Here it is in full: 

“The assertion that approximately 75% of China’s consumption is domestically produced as of April 2025—rests on overly reductive reasoning that underestimates the complexity of China’s integration into the global economy. While it is true that China has pursued policies aimed at bolstering self-reliance, particularly under the “dual circulation” strategy, the claim fails to account for the intricate dependencies embedded within China’s manufacturing apparatus.

First, China’s domestic production is inextricably reliant on imported raw materials, intermediate goods, and capital-intensive technologies—many of which originate from advanced economies. To claim 75% self-sufficiency in consumption glosses over the embedded foreign value added in goods labeled as “domestically produced.” According to OECD TiVA (Trade in Value Added) data, a significant portion of China’s exports—and by extension, domestic consumption—contains foreign inputs, particularly in high-tech sectors.

Second, the notion of “domestic production” is analytically flawed unless it distinguishes between final assembly and genuine end-to-end domestic value creation. Much of China’s manufacturing prowess remains focused on assembly, often drawing from global supply chains, especially in sectors such as electronics, pharmaceuticals, and automotive components.

Finally, even if domestic production accounts for a numerical majority of consumption in sheer volume, such a metric fails to capture the qualitative dependence on imports for high-value, strategic, or technologically advanced goods. Hence, a robust evaluation of China’s economic autonomy must go beyond surface-level statistics and address the structural entanglements that belie any simplistic percentage-based claims.”

text:
The path towards sobriety might just begin with an out-of-body experience. ⁠
⁠
A team at University College London is researching how effective a one-off dose of DMT could be in managing problematic alcohol consumption. DMT – the active ingredient in ayahuasca – is one the most powerful naturally occurring psychedelic substances. ⁠
⁠
"Excessive drinking is partly driven by alcohol hijacking the brain’s built-in motivation and reward system,” said Professor Ravi Das, who is co-leading the trial. “We are seeking to counteract that with our treatment.”⁠
⁠
Trial participants will receive either an intravenous dose of the drug (which, the team has confirmed, is just as intense as it sounds) or a placebo. They’ll also be given MRI brain scans while watching film excerpts containing drinking, including scenes from ‘The Trip’ and ‘Withnail and I’, and then tracked for nine months to see if and how the drug works.⁠
⁠
With 120 study participants, The Guardian reports it will be the largest psychedelic brain imaging study to date.

David Lynch, the visionary mind behind 'Twin Peaks' and 'Mulholland Drive', has passed away at 78. ⁠
⁠
His work was so singular (and often so uniquely unsettling) that he earned the rare distinction of having his name turned into an adjective. Films like 'Eraserhead', 'Blue Velvet', and 'Lost Highway' defined the Lynchian style – a surreal blend of the macabre, the mysterious, and the mundane – and profoundly influenced a generation of filmmakers. ⁠
⁠
Lynch's family announced his death today, writing, "There’s a big hole in the world now that he’s no longer with us. But, as he would say, 'Keep your eye on the donut and not on the hole.'"

text:
In the late 90s and early 2000s, Buddyhead was the best place to hang out on the internet, a music and mud-slinging gossip blog at the heart of an exhilarating (yet weirdly nameless) global counterculture. This DIY punk scene predated indie sleaze, was repulsed by nu-metal, and hoisted aloft bands—The Icarus Line, At the Drive-In—that left the competition looking bad and dumb.⁠
⁠
Buddyhead was run by Travis Keller and Icarus Line guitarist Aaron North, a pair of piss-takers from Los Angeles who rapidly earned notoriety with a series of pranks on the era’s biggest musicians. Most famous was the time Keller stole three of Fred Durst’s treasured red baseball caps from his office before auctioning them off for a rape charity, after a woman was assaulted during Limp Bizkit’s set at Woodstock ’99.⁠
⁠
Happily, for Buddyhead’s many, many enemies, social media and music streaming platforms saw the site fizzle out. Keller spent six years on heroin, nearly dying, but he overcame his addiction and now @buddyhead_ is enjoying a second life. Meanwhile, Keller is raising money to finish ‘On The Lash,’ a definitive documentary of the raucous cultural moment he lived through from 1998 to 2005.⁠
⁠
Watch the trailer in the carousel above, learn about their crowdfunder, then read VICE writer @yungtolstoi’s full interview with Travis Keller through the link in bio.⁠
⁠
Photographs in slides 1, 5 and 6 by @stphnnclsmth

text:
Bill Maher spoke with American politician and farmer Sen. Jon Tester on 'Real Time with Bill Maher' about the current and ongoing bird flu epidemic in the USA.

Here's why we need to be concerned about bird flu:

👉 Bird flu, which most prominently affects farmed birds, has been spreading among dairy cows throughout the US.

👉 The US has seen almost 100 cases of cats becoming sick or dying from bird flu since 2022. At least a dozen died after drinking raw milk from infected cows on a dairy farm. It may be possible for cats to transmit the virus to humans.

👉 While there is no evidence of human-to-human transmission of bird flu, there have been several human cases documented, particularly among agricultural workers.

👉 In January of 2025 US officials confirmed that a person in Louisiana became the first human to die with H5N1 bird flu in the country. 

👉 Bird flu can cause symptoms including eye irritation and fever in humans. In some cases, it can be fatal. While human infection is rare, there are concerns that the virus could mutate, allowing for human-to-human transmission. Experts have described bird flu as a “ticking time bomb” for a future pandemic.

text:
Please don’t eat butter sticks.

NEW STUDY FINDINGS:

A new independent study has found that replacing butter with plant-based oils may reduce the risk of premature death.

Comment ‘butter’ on this post & we will DM you an article about the study. 

The research analyzed 33 years of data for 221,054 US adults and found that the people who consumed the highest amounts of butter had a higher risk of death from all causes than those who consumed the lowest amounts, including cancer and cardiovascular disease.

Those with the highest consumption of butter had a 15 percent higher risk of death than those with the lowest, while each additional 10 grams of butter (approximately one tablespoon) per day was associated with a 12 percent increased risk of death from cancer.

Conversely, the research linked the consumption of plant-based oils – particularly olive, canola (rapeseed), and soybean – with a reduced risk of premature death from all causes. Furthermore, those who substituted approximately one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of plant-based oil benefited from a 17 percent reduction in risk of death.

Read more on the PBN website.

British actor Jason Isaacs has revealed that he follows a plant-based diet. 

Isaacs is one of the stars of season three of the hit HBO series The White Lotus, which has just released its fifth episode. He plays the character Timothy Ratliff, a wealthy businessman vacationing with his wife and three children.

The series is filmed in Koh Samui, an island in the Gulf of Thailand known for its tropical landscapes, stunning beaches, and luxury resorts. While Thailand is a popular destination for vegan travelers, Isaacs told the Evening Standard that he struggled to find animal-free food in the specific area he was staying in. 

“I’m a vegan. Everyone said to me, ‘Thailand’s gonna be amazing for you!’ It turned out to be the opposite,” he said. “Being a vegan out there is a nightmare. Even the hotels don’t really cater for it. I ended up eating a variation on the same dish nearly every day: some version of tofu curry.”

text:
April 8 is International Romani Day

Romanistan honored to partner with @feminist to celebrate Romani culture, resilience, and resistance—and to raise awareness of the human rights struggles Roma continue to face.

For centuries, Roma, a diasporic Indian ethnic group, have survived slavery, genocide, forced migration, and systemic racism. Today, we continue to resist through art, activism, education, and community care.

Swipe through our collaborative post to learn more about who the Roma are, why “Gypsy” is a slur, and how you can support Romani liberation.

🎙️ @romanistanpodcast is one of many resources created by and for Roma, sharing stories, interviews, and cultural insight from across the diaspora. Listen wherever you get podcasts. 

📚 We’ve also highlighted powerful Romani-run organizations doing the work: 

Giuvlipen @giuvlipen
Phiren Amenca @PhirenAmenca 
Veveripen @ververipen 
Romnja Feminist Library @romnjafeministlibrary 
Feminist Collective of Romani Gender Experts @Romanigenderexperts
European Roma Rights Center (ERRC) @errcinsta 
European Roma Institute for Arts and Culture (ERIAC)
E-Romnja @eromnja12 
RomArchive @romarchive_berlin
Fundacja w Stronę Dialogu @fundacjawstronedialogu

Follow, support, donate, and share.
We are still here, still dreaming, still fighting. 🧿✨💚❤️💙☸️

Song is “Roma Stars” on Tehno Vrăjitoarele, the first Romani feminist trap album, by Kali & Niko G, featuring Bianca. All are members of Giuvlipen.

Support abortion access and Black women in Texas by donating to our campaign for the @theafiyacenter’s SYS Fund.

The SYS Fund—Supporting Your Sistahs—was created in response to urgent calls from Black women in Texas being dismissed, denied, or delayed when seeking abortion care. Powered by @theafiyacenter, it offers mutual aid and practical support—covering everything from procedures to travel, lodging, childcare, and aftercare.

text:
On April 5, millions of people across all 50 U.S. states—and in cities across Europe—took to the streets for the Hands Off! protests, a sweeping show of resistance against the country’s hostile takeover of rights and freedoms by Donald Trump and Elon Musk. 🎞️ by @vuralelibol, @tenilletaggart, @perfectunion

Over 1,300 events were held nationwide, with turnout in cities like Boston, Washington D.C., and New York exceeding 100,000. The cross-movement mobilization was organized by Indivisible (@indivisibleteam), MoveOn (@moveon), and dozens of grassroots allies, and brought together civil rights organizations, labor unions, pro-democracy coalitions, and local advocates. 

Millions were united by a shared belief: that Trump’s authoritarian power grab must be stopped—and that those who are aiding and abetting it must be held accountable. 

Protesters rallied in defense of public schools, national parks, veterans’ healthcare, LGBTQ+ rights, reproductive freedom, and fair elections. They marched against billionaire oligarchs, dark money, corporate greed, the deportation of legal immigrants, and the gutting of federal agencies by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). Trump is making life harder for everyday people while benefiting his richest allies—and people across the country are refusing to stand by.

Hands Off! was more than a protest—it was a unified call to protect what matters most: our freedoms, our communities, and our future.

Sources: Axios, The New York Times, Common Dreams, CNN

HERE’S HOW TO KEEP THE MOMENTUM GOING: 
👉 Contact Your Representatives: Call, email, or write to your representatives and demand bold opposition to Project 2025 and the MAGA agenda. @5calls
👉 Stay informed. Follow @FEMINIST for intersectional feminist news and updates.
👉 Support organizations like FEMINIST who are fighting for your voice to be heard. Giving $5 right now can help us continue the fight and remain a source of information for activists and changemakers everywhere.

text:
🏆 FEMINIST WIN 🏆 Alabama has been blocked from prosecuting those aiding Alabama residents in seeking abortion care! 🎞️ by @slavic.queer

In a major victory for reproductive rights, U.S. District Judge Myron Thompson ruled that Alabama cannot prosecute individuals or organizations assisting residents in traveling out of state for legal abortion care. The case, brought by the Yellowhammer Fund (@yellowfund), successfully blocked Attorney General Steve Marshall’s attempt to use criminal conspiracy laws against those providing support. The court affirmed that such prosecution would violate constitutional rights, including free speech and interstate travel.

This ruling restores the ability of healthcare providers and advocacy groups to offer vital assistance—such as information, logistical aid, and financial support—without fear of legal retaliation. In Alabama, where abortion is banned at any stage of pregnancy with no exceptions for rape or incest, many are forced to seek care elsewhere. Now, they can do so with help.

Sources: The Guardian, AP News

TAKE ACTION: 
👉 Support local Alabama abortion funds including @yellowfund and @alabamacohosh
👉 Need abortion care or resources? Visit YouAlwaysHaveOptions.com for legal protections, abortion pills by mail, travel assistance, and more.
👉 Learn more about abortion rights in the U.S., your state, and globally, in our FEMINIST toolkit to protect abortion acces: feminists.co/discover/reproductive-freedom-bsmph

More than 8,000 firefighters and emergency responders have been battling the LA fires that broke out on Tuesday. ⁠
⁠
Earlier this week, the Los Angeles Fire Department asked all off-duty crews to assist in the operation, the first time they’ve had to do so in 19 years. Crews from around California, plus teams and equipment from Arizona, New Mexico, Nevada, Washington, Idaho and Oregon have been drafted in to help, while Mexico and Canada have been pitching in with supplies, personnel and water-bombing aircraft.⁠

Nearly 800 of the firefighters deployed by California are inmates from state prisons, who earn just $5.80 to $10.24 a day, plus an additional $1 per hour when responding to active emergencies.
⁠
The firefighters are facing extreme and unprecedented conditions, with hydrants running dry and hurricane-force winds causing the fires to spread rapidly. The hope is that calmer winds today will help crews get the situation under control, but LA Fire Chief Kristin Crowley has warned that Los Angeles is still "not out of danger".

Photos via Getty Images

South Korea’s Constitutional Court has unanimously upheld the impeachment of President Yoon Suk Yeol, permanently removing him from office following his unconstitutional declaration of martial law in December 2024. The crowd outside the court erupted in celebration as the verdict was announced, with supporters of the impeachment reacting with tears, hugs, and cheers. The decision reflects the power of sustained public pressure. 🇰🇷 🎞️ by @julie_y00n for @washingtonpost

💖 For more stories and wins, follow @FEMINIST and subscribe to FEMINIST’s newsletter—delivered straight to your inbox every week: feminists.co/news

The court’s ruling comes after mass protests erupted nationwide, with citizens vehemently opposing Yoon’s six-hour imposition of martial law—an extreme measure that grants military authority over civilian functions, including law enforcement and governance. Public demonstrations played a critical role in pressuring lawmakers and the judiciary to act.

Prime Minister Han Duck-soo has assumed the role of acting president, providing interim leadership. A new presidential election must be held within 60 days, as required by South Korea’s constitution. The upcoming vote marks a pivotal moment for the country and underscores the role of civic engagement in safeguarding constitutional order and preventing authoritarian overreach. 

Sources: AP News, The Washington Post

text:
The murals on Salt Lake City’s Fleet Block, located at 300 West and 800 South, featured portraits of individuals who were murdered by law enforcement. 🎞️ by @sltrib, @franciscokjolseth, @netmoser

In total, 26 faces were memorialized in these artworks including George Floyd and Breonna Taylor, whose deaths sparked national protests in 2020. The murals also honored Utah residents such as Bernardo Palacios-Carbajal, Zane James, Cindreia Europe, Bobby Ray Duckworth, Dillon Taylor, and Jovany Mercado-Bedolla. These portraits served as a powerful memorial and gathering place for the community to honor and remember those lost. 

However, as of March 2025, the buildings housing these murals have been demolished to make way for redevelopment. The city plans to incorporate a dedicated memorial and commissioned artwork in the new development to honor the history and social justice themes represented by the original murals.

“We hoped that the mural would be the change,” said Mercado, whose 26-year-old brother, Jovany Mercado-Bedolla, was shot and killed by police in August 2019. “To see them come down, [we’re] feeling defeated. Now we’re just hoping the city stays true to their word.”

Source: The Salt Lake Tribune

text:
This week, @FEMINIST spoke with PeiYao, President & CEO of Global Fund for Women (@globalfundwomen), about the urgent need for gender justice funding, the power of unrestricted grants, and how real-time data is shaping feminist movements worldwide.

Despite progress, feminist organizations continue to face severe funding gaps, receiving only 1.9% of all U.S. charitable giving in 2024. To bridge this gap, Global Fund for Women provides unrestricted, multi-year grants to grassroots groups tackling issues from climate justice to gender-based violence. PeiYao emphasizes that trusting feminist leaders with flexible funding is key to sustainable change.

The conversation also highlights the Gender Justice Data Hub, a groundbreaking tool monitoring feminist mobilization in real time. Since 2023, it has helped Global Fund for Women rapidly direct resources to movements fighting for land rights, democracy, maternity leave, and legal protections against femicide and sexual violence.

Global Fund for Women is a leading funder of gender justice organizations, initiatives, and movements worldwide. Since 1987, Global Fund for Women has given nearly $240 million in grants to more than 5,000 organizations in 179 countries.

💌 Subscribe to the @feminist newsletter for more #FeministFeatures: feminists.co/news

text:
Everyday necessities. Personal care products. Medical supplies. Essential goods marketed towards women often come with a higher price tag due to discriminatory import taxes known as ‘pink tariffs.’

These aren’t just numbers on a spreadsheet. They’re lived realities for women across the country, single mothers, low-income earners, caregivers, women of color, who bear the brunt of a system designed without them in mind.

Why should a mother pay more for her child’s clothing? Why should menstrual products be taxed like luxury goods? Why are our voices ignored in trade policy discussions?

It’s time to make the invisible visible. To challenge these discriminatory practices. To demand policy change that values economic equity for all.

Let’s break the silence around pink tariffs and advocate for a fairer, more just economic landscape.

text:
This 87-year-old feminist remains undeterred, protesting pipeline projects, corporate greed, and government inaction! 📣 🌎 🪧

📨 Subscribe to the @feminist newsletter to discover more leaders in #Herstory

Jane Fonda @janefonda (b. 1937) is an award-winning actress, political activist, and feminist who has spent decades using her platform for change. Born in New York City, she trained at the Actors Studio and launched her film career in the 1960s with Tall Story and Barefoot in the Park.

From opposing the Vietnam War in the 1970s to being a vocal opponent of President Trump’s, Fonda has championed civil rights, women’s rights, and climate justice—clashing with the establishment at every turn. Inspired by Naomi Klein and Greta Thunberg, she launched Fire Drill Fridays, a weekly protest on Capitol Hill to demand urgent action on the climate crisis, facing arrest multiple times at age 82 alongside Indigenous activists and youth organizers.

With two Oscars, seven Golden Globes, and five arrests (and counting), Fonda has never shied away from a fight—or from handcuffs. Her activism hasn’t slowed with age—it’s only grown bolder. 

Sources: Vogue, PBS, NPR, Time, Marie Claire  janefonda.com, janepac.com

text:
We need to make motherhood aspirational again.

The modern world is anti-motherhood because you can’t slap a price tag on it… and if you can’t monetize it, it doesn’t count.

In our hypercapitalist clown world, everything is measured by how much money it makes us.

Mothers staying at home, on the other hand, is a loss...

But what if moms did send a bill at the end of your life? Imagine an invoice for every sleepless night, home cooked meal, and ounce of love? You’d be in eternal debt.

Turns out, motherhood isn’t just valuable, it’s the most valuable job in the world. And we’re going to continue to suffer until we realize this.

Touting its foundational values of hard work, honesty, and integrity, drugstore giant Walgreens confirmed Wednesday it is proud of its origins as a small business that in today’s economy would absolutely never have been able to get off the ground. “Here at Walgreens, we still live by the same commitment to customer service preached by Charles Walgreen when he opened his first store back in 1901,” said company president Alex Gourlay, referring to the miniscule, independent pharmacy that, if opened in the modern era, would quickly be crushed by the present-day Walgreens and other retail chains whose outsized bargaining power allows them to squeeze out would-be competitors at will. 

“Just like Charles, we take great pride in everything we do. We now have more than 8,000 locations, but in many ways, we’re still that same little shop on the corner [that in the current market would be boarded up within 12 months, leaving its bankrupt owner hundreds of thousands of dollars in debt].” Gourlay added that each Walgreens store sees itself as an integral part of the community it serves, in the sense that residents no longer have any other place to shop and are thus completely dependent upon it.

text:
🏆 FEMINIST WIN 🏆 Portugal has finally outlawed child marriage, raising its minimum legal age for marriage to 18 years old without exceptions.

The new law aims to end child, early, and forced marriages in Portugal so that everybody who enters a marriage is a consenting adult. Prior to this change, Portuguese children aged 16 and 17 could be legally married with parental consent. Any marriages of 16 and 17-year-olds that took place before the new law took effect will still remain valid under Portuguese law.

Globally, an estimated 12 million girls are married before the age of 18 every year. Girls Not Brides, a global partnership working to end child marriage, has stated that laws against child marriage are important, but not enough to end child marriage on their own. Evidence shows that criminalizing child marriage alone can have unintended negative consequences for child brides and their families. To further prevent child marriage, countries must establish policies to address the root causes of child marriage, such as poverty, gender inequality, and lack of access to education.

Source: Girls Not Brides

Gustavo Petro added that coke is only "illegal because it's made in Latin America".⁠
⁠
During a ministerial meeting, the Colombian president – who has been working to end six decades of conflict between the country's armed groups since taking office in 2022 – said, "If you want peace, you must dismantle the drug trade. It could be easily dismantled if cocaine were legalized globally. It would be sold like wine."⁠
⁠
Many experts would agree on that point. The global illegal drug market is estimated to be worth around $500 billion every year. Take that income stream away from drug cartels and you take away much of the power they wield and the harm they’re able to inflict. ⁠
⁠
As for whether coke is worse than whiskey, it's a little more complicated. Neither are good for you, obviously – and while alcohol kills more people than all other drugs combined, it's also the most readily available. Cocaine is considered to be more dangerous in the short-term, and can also lead to serious long-term health conditions.⁠
⁠
Still, it's refreshing to see at least one world leader acknowledge that the War on Drugs is the massive waste of time that it truly is.

text:
Please, enjoy these lovely paintings of some angry little dogs. ⁠
⁠
Artist Robert Roest says he was inspired to start the series, titled 'Indoor feelings, and single-door kennels in your soul, after getting lost in a "picture, Reels and meme rabbit hole of snarling dogs" during lockdown. ⁠
⁠
"As a younger person I loved to paint very realistically, and I loved the old masters," he says. "After art academy I thought that was all passé. But with these pictures I got a reason to paint like that again, and juxtapose it with digital aesthetics – the snapshot compositions, the motion blur of a maw, teeth or tongue, and sometimes the lit-up eyes due to cameras not being able to capture light well, resulting in these dogs appearing possessed."⁠
⁠
He adds, "A lot of the dogs I paint might just be yawning or playing, but partly due to camera failures they appear evil - while in reality they might be very sweet. I'm generally interested in the deceptive quality of pictures and images, and often reflect on and play with that in my work."⁠
⁠
@robert.roest

text:
All charges have been dropped against Selena Maria Chandler-Scott, a 24-year-old woman in Georgia who was arrested last month after suffering a miscarriage.

On March 20, paramedics responded to a 911 call and found her unconscious and bleeding. She was taken to the hospital, but after a witness reported that she had placed the fetal remains in a dumpster, Chandler-Scott was charged with “concealing the death of another” and “abandonment of a dead body,” facing up to 13 years in prison. At the hospital, it was determined she had miscarried at around 19 weeks, and her charges were dropped.

Her arrest marked the first known attempt by Georgia prosecutors to use the state’s “fetal personhood law” to criminally charge someone for pregnancy loss. Since the overturning of Roe v. Wade in 2022, criminal charges against pregnant people have spiked, with at least 210 cases in just 2023 — 22 involving miscarriages or stillbirths.

This case sparked public outrage across the U.S. and serves as a reminder that collective organizing and action work. Nonprofits, medical professionals, advocacy groups, and the people came to the defense of the woman wrongfully charged— and in the end, the State of Georgia dropped all pending charges against her. 💖

Sources: The Guardian, The CUT, NBC News

text:
"The Pardon of the Camper Vans" – a blessing for you and your motorhome. ⁠
⁠
Every year, hundreds of RV-owning Catholics drive from all over France to the small village of Malestroit. There, priests bless them, their vehicles and their dogs, to help ensure safe travels in the year ahead.⁠
⁠
The event is part of the wider Pardons Bretons, an ancestral celebration involving over 2,000 processional pilgrimages in Brittany. Romain Ruiz (@romainruiz_), who took these photos, says it's an inventive modern way for the Catholic Church to lure people into its flock.⁠
⁠
“This benediction is only seven years old, [and] I’m really interested in how ancient rituals can adapt themselves to reach new people here in our time,” he explains. “This was the perfect example of how the pardon could take new forms to attract new people into the Church."

text:
Over 100 Reddit communities have banned links to X.com following Elon Musk’s much-discussed arm gesture at Trump’s inauguration.⁠
⁠
Subreddits for Formula 1 (4.8 million members), Dungeons & Dragons (4 million), the NBA (15 million), and the NFL (12 million) are just some of the communities to have banned X/Twitter from their pages, with hundreds more considering the move. These pages are almost always run by fans, of course, rather than the official institutions, but it’s a mark of just how fed up many people are with the world's richest man.⁠
⁠
The controversy stems from Musk extending his arm twice in a gesture that has been described as a Nazi salute. Musk responded to the criticism by saying, "Frankly, they need better dirty tricks. The 'everyone is Hitler' attack is sooo tired."⁠
⁠
Reddit itself has clarified that it hasn't banned X links site-wide, saying, “Reddit has a longstanding commitment to freedom of speech and freedom of association.”
